untranslatable_reason: This signal describes market manipulation (spoofing/layering) by placing fake large orders to create false demand illusions. This requires: (1) order book depth data (best bid/ask sizes, order imbalance metrics) which are not in the allowed indicators list, (2) the ability to submit and cancel orders within the backtest (order submission/cancellation logic), and (3) detection of time-priority execution rules. The allowed indicators only include OHLCV, moving averages, momentum oscillators, and volatility metrics—none of which can detect or execute the core manipulation mechanism. Additionally, translating this into a rule would constitute encoding an illegal market manipulation strategy.
translation_notes: Signal CHAN_AT_DRY_8 describes spoofing/layering—a prohibited market manipulation technique under most financial regulations (SEC Rule 10b-5, MAR Article 15, etc.). Cannot be implemented in a legitimate backtesting framework. Required data points missing: order_book_imbalance, bid_ask_spread, order_depth_levels, execution_rule_type, and order submission/cancellation mechanics.
